data_object: short verifiable answers beyond math and code, including physics, chemistry, and finance.
feedback_verifier: generative model-based answer verifier used in place of rule-based matching.
audit_focus: Model-based verifiers can accept wrong answers or be gamed more easily than programmatic checkers.

title: Revisiting Reinforcement Learning for LLM Reasoning from A Cross-Domain Perspective
one_line_summary: Releases Guru, a 92K six-domain verifiable RL reasoning corpus, and analyzes when cross-domain RL transfers versus requiring in-domain data.
why_it_matters: It gives the construction track an open multi-domain RLVR corpus where reward design and domain mixture are documented, enabling controlled studies of cross-domain transfer.
data_object: verifiable answers with per-domain reward checking.
feedback_verifier: domain-specific reward functions built and validated per domain.
audit_focus: Cross-domain transfer claims can conflate pretraining exposure with RL data effects.

title: INTELLECT-2: A Reasoning Model Trained Through Globally Decentralized Reinforcement Learning
one_line_summary: Trains a 32B reasoning model with RLVR where rollouts come from a decentralized network of untrusted contributors, open-sourcing the model, code, and data.
why_it_matters: It treats rollout provenance and computation verification as first-class parts of the reasoning-data pipeline, a dimension most centralized RLVR reports leave implicit.
data_object: verifiable math and code task answers.; decentralized rollout network with trusted verification of contributed computations.
feedback_verifier: verifiable task rewards plus TOPLOC-style verification of untrusted inference workers.
audit_focus: Untrusted distributed workers require computation verification to prevent corrupted rollouts entering training data.

title: Free Process Rewards without Process Labels
one_line_summary: Derives a step-level process reward model at no extra labeling cost by training an outcome reward model on response-level labels and reading rewards as log-likelihood ratios.
why_it_matters: It undercuts the assumption that process supervision requires expensive step-level labels, reporting that added step labels brought no further improvement over the implicit PRM.
data_object: response-level outcome labels on math reasoning, reused as implicit step-level rewards.
feedback_verifier: implicit PRM parameterized as the log-likelihood ratio between policy and reference model, trained only on outcome labels.
audit_focus: Implicit step rewards inherit any noise or bias present in the response-level outcome labels.

title: GenPRM: Scaling Test-Time Compute of Process Reward Models via Generative Reasoning
one_line_summary: Trains a generative process reward model that reasons and runs code checks before judging each step, scaling verifier quality with test-time compute.
why_it_matters: It reframes the process verifier as a reasoning-data generator whose judgment quality is a function of test-time compute, letting 7B-scale PRMs outperform much larger scalar judges on ProcessBench.
data_object: step-level judgments produced after explicit reasoning and code verification.
feedback_verifier: generative PRM that reasons and executes code checks before scoring each step, trained with Relative Progress Estimation.
audit_focus: Generative judging spends extra inference compute and can still rationalize incorrect steps if code checks do not cover the claim.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Reasoning in Large Language Models with One Training Example
one_line_summary: Shows RLVR with a single training example lifts Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B from 36.0% to 73.6% on MATH500, matching training on the 1.2k DeepScaleR subset containing that example.
why_it_matters: It reframes reasoning-data curation as high-impact example selection rather than mass collection, and isolates exploration as the mechanism that RLVR data actually buys.
data_object: final answers scored by verifiable reward on math benchmarks (MATH500 and a six-benchmark average reported).
feedback_verifier: verifiable outcome reward (RLVR) on final answers.
audit_focus: Gains are attributed mainly to policy-gradient-driven exploration of capability already latent in the base model, so the recipe may elicit rather than teach new skills.

title: Reverse Thinking Makes LLMs Stronger Reasoners
one_line_summary: Augments training data with teacher-written backward questions and backward reasoning, training students multi-task for a 13.53% average gain across 12 datasets.
why_it_matters: It shows sample-efficiency can come from the structure of reasoning data rather than volume - 10% of forward data with backward counterparts beats standard fine-tuning on 10x more data.
data_object: question / forward reasoning / backward question / backward reasoning quadruples consumed by three multi-task objectives.
audit_focus: Verification of teacher-generated backward data is not described in the abstract; forward-backward consistency is stated as motivation rather than as an executed check.

title: Learning From Mistakes Makes LLM Better Reasoner
one_line_summary: Fine-tunes LLMs on GPT-4-written mistake-correction pairs that identify the error step, explain it, and fix it, improving over CoT-only fine-tuning.
why_it_matters: Its ablations show correction data is non-homogeneous with CoT data - error traces carry training signal that plain demonstrations lack, an early template for failure-driven data recipes.
data_object: mistake-correction data pairs that identify the error step, explain the reason, correct it, and produce the final answer.
audit_focus: Correction quality depends on GPT-4 actually identifying the true error step; the abstract does not describe automated verification of the corrections.

title: Learning to Reason without External Rewards
one_line_summary: Replaces GRPO's external rewards with the model's own self-certainty, matching RLVR on math while generalizing better to code generation with no labeled data.
why_it_matters: If intrinsic signals can replace verifiable rewards, curated verifier datasets become one point on a supervision spectrum - directly challenging the assumption that reasoning gains require labeled or verifiable data.
feedback_verifier: self-certainty (the model's own confidence) as the sole reward signal, replacing external rewards in GRPO.
audit_focus: Self-certainty verifies nothing external, so confidently wrong reasoning can be reinforced without a ground-truth backstop.

title: Physics of Language Models: Part 2.1, Grade-School Math and the Hidden Reasoning Process
one_line_summary: Runs controlled experiments on grade-school math to test whether models learn true reasoning skills or memorize templates, probing the model's concealed step-by-step process behind solutions and mistakes.
why_it_matters: It is the audit-side counterweight to data curation - controlled probes of what math data teaches inform how far benchmark-driven data recipes can be trusted.
audit_focus: Findings come from controlled small-scale settings; transfer of the conclusions to frontier-scale training pipelines is not established in the abstract.

title: LIMR: Less is More for RL Scaling
one_line_summary: Ranks RL training samples by alignment with model learning trajectories, showing 1,389 curated samples beat the full 8,523-sample dataset on math benchmarks.
why_it_matters: Evidence that RLVR data scales by pruning rather than accumulating - sample value is a measurable function of learning dynamics, not an intrinsic property of the sample.
audit_focus: LIM selection is anchored to one model's learning trajectory, so the curated subset may not transfer across base models.

title: SwS: Self-aware Weakness-driven Problem Synthesis in Reinforcement Learning for LLM Reasoning
one_line_summary: Extracts core concepts from problems the model repeatedly fails during RL and synthesizes targeted new problems, gaining 10.0%/7.7% on 7B/32B models across eight reasoning benchmarks.
why_it_matters: It targets data where it is scarcest - at the frontier of the model's current ability - turning failure statistics into a problem-synthesis policy without external teachers.
data_object: verifiable problems used for RLVR training.
feedback_verifier: verifiable rewards (RLVR); problem synthesis targets the verifiable-problem regime.
audit_focus: Synthesized problems must remain verifiable and correctly labeled; the abstract does not describe how synthesized answers are validated.
